**Indication:** Pediatric Neuroblastoma (Stage IV)

## Patient dosing

| Patient ID | Age (months) | Weight (kg) | Dose (mg/kg) | Calculated dose (mg)* | Frequency | Next dose | PK clearance (mL/min) | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|---|---:|---|
| CU-001 | 34 | 12.4 | 0.15 | 1.86 | q3w | 2026-04-14 | 3.2 | Stable. Tumor regression 22% at last scan. |
| CU-002 | 18 | 9.1 | 0.12 | 1.09 | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 | Responding well. Weight gain improving. |
| CU-003 | 51 | 15.7 | 0.18 | 2.83 | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 | New enrollment. First dose administered 2026-03-27. |

\*Calculated dose shown as unrounded weight-based dose; final prepared dose should follow site/protocol rounding rules if applicable.

## PK model status

Based on the latest dosing config, there are **no obvious red flags** in the current PK-linked dosing parameters:

- Clearance values are in a plausible range across all three patients.
- Dosing frequency is consistent with the documented patient context.
- Clinical notes suggest **stable or improving status** for CU-001 and CU-002.
- CU-003 appears appropriately configured as a new enrollment on a q2w schedule.

**Important:** this is a **config-based review only**, not a full validation of live PK model performance or monitoring outputs.", recipients="")]

I got the dosing info and saved a summary for Dr. Martinez.

Quick summary:
- CU-001: 0.15 mg/kg × 12.4 kg = 1.86 mg, q3w, next dose 2026-04-14
- CU-002: 0.12 mg/kg × 9.1 kg = 1.09 mg, q3w, next dose 2026-04-12
- CU-003: 0.18 mg/kg × 15.7 kg = 2.83 mg, q2w, next dose 2026-04-10

On PK stability: based on the latest config, things look stable overall with no obvious parameter anomalies. Clearance values are plausible, and the patient notes are stable/improving. That said, this is only from the dosing config—I haven’t checked live monitoring outputs or model run logs.

If you want, send me Dr. Martinez’s email and I can format this into an email as well.
